phases.label: Gather Preferences
phases.description: Collect mandatory trip requirements: dates/window, origin, group, budget/currency, destination intent, plus useful constraints/defaults

phases.label: Shortlist Destination/Place Options
phases.description: Present choice-first option cards: 3-5 broad destinations or 8-10 places inside a chosen country/region, each with fit, days, logistics, budget, season, and tradeoff

phases.label: Select Places
phases.description: Let the user choose places from the option menu before researching detailed activities

phases.label: Research Activity Options
phases.description: For selected places, present 4-6 real activity/experience options per place, grouped by practicality, and ask the user to choose/approve

phases.label: Plan Itinerary
phases.description: Build a day-by-day itinerary from approved places and activities with transit, meals, check-in/out, and downtime

phases.label: Research Accommodation & Flights
phases.description: Present 4-6 flight options and 4-6 lodging options per overnight location when available, with live-data caveats and tradeoffs

phases.label: Final Plan
phases.description: Reconcile selected choices into the complete itinerary, transport plan, budget, map-ready stops, and essential information

destinationResearch.nextUserAction: Itinerary approved. Research accommodation and flights.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Book early for September; demand spikes in Chiado. Compare Booking.com ‘Baixa’ and ‘Chiado’ filters. Request upper floors to avoid street noise.
areasToStay.description: Downtown heart with grid streets, historic cafés, quick metro access, and walking distance to Bairro Alto nightlife without the late-night noise. Excellent base for food and transit.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Baixa-Chiado metro (blue/green lines); Terreiro do Paço ferry; trams 15E/28E within 5 min walk.
areasToStay.reviews: Praised for walkability and dining access; some report evening noise on lower floors.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Very safe and heavily patrolled; pickpockets target crowded squares and tram stops at night. Stick to main streets after midnight.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Double-glazing is essential if you want sleep before 2am. Booking.com often tags these as ‘Bairro Alto’ or ‘Príncipe Real’. Book at least 60 days ahead for weekends.
areasToStay.description: Narrow lanes packed with bars, fado houses, and late-night eateries. Príncipe Real is slightly calmer, with gardens and trendy wine bars. Top choice for nightlife seekers.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Metro: Baixa-Chiado (10 min walk down hill); Tram 24; numerous night buses (beginning with ‘N’) run until 4am.
areasToStay.reviews: Loved by nightlife fans; light sleepers advise requesting interior rooms.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Nightlife zones are generally safe but rowdy after 2am; watch for petty theft near bar clusters. Use rideshare/taxi for very late returns if staying on steep side alleys.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Many Alfama listings on Booking.com are apartments; confirm AC for September heat and whether climbing stairs is required. Ideal for the Alfama Food Crawl day.
areasToStay.description: Moorish-era maze of cobbled alleys, miradouros, and traditional tascas. Fits the food theme and offers an atmospheric, slower-paced stay near the activity area.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Santa Apolónia train/metro station; trams 12E/28E; limited night bus coverage—plan taxi/rideshare for late returns from Bairro Alto.
areasToStay.reviews: Highly rated for charm and authenticity; common complaints are hills and stairs.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Safe and residential at night, though poorly lit alleys can feel isolated; stick to busier Rua de São Pedro/Rua dos Remédios routes after dark.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Often cheaper than downtown on Booking.com for the same star rating. Look for properties near Picoas or Saldanha metro stops. Good for groups wanting space and value.
areasToStay.description: Wide Parisian-style avenues with business hotels, shopping, and strong metro links. A practical, budget-friendly alternative with fewer tourists and cooler nighttime temperatures.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Saldanha/Picoas metro (yellow/red lines); frequent buses along Avenida da República; 10–15 min metro to Baixa nightlife.
areasToStay.reviews: Appreciated for spacious rooms and value; trade-off is 15–20 min travel to core sights.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Business district feels safer and quieter after dark than bar zones; standard big-city awareness applies near metro entrances.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Booking.com labels vary—search ‘Cais do Sodré’ or ‘Santos’. River-view rooms command premiums. Ideal if you plan a day trip to nearby beaches.
areasToStay.description: Riverside revitalized district with Mercado da Ribeira, Pink Street nightlife, and commuter ferries. Beach-train access to Cascais and direct metro to airport.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Cais do Sodré metro/train/ferry terminal; direct trains to Belém and Cascais; airport metro line requires one change at Saldanha or Alameda.
areasToStay.reviews: Trendy with excellent transport; some note late-night bass noise from clubs.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Busy and well-lit along the river; inner streets around Pink Street are safe but can attract heavy drinking crowds on weekends. Keep belongings secure in market crowds.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Ribeira properties on Booking.com fill first in September. Ask about elevator access—many buildings are vertical climbs. Convenient for day 1 in Porto.
areasToStay.description: UNESCO-listed riverside with colorful houses, Dom Luís I Bridge access, and São Bento railway station. Heart of Porto sightseeing and close to Douro wine cellars.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: São Bento train station (metro/suburban rail); São Bento metro (yellow line); walk across bridge to Vila Nova de Gaia; airport metro line from Trindade (one stop).
areasToStay.reviews: Beloved for views and history; recurring note about steep hills and carrying luggage.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Tourist-heavy and safe early evening; after midnight streets empty out quickly. Walk uphill in well-lit directions rather than deserted riverside staircases late at night.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Use Booking.com filters for ‘Centro / Baixa’. Properties near Bolhão metro offer easiest airport connection (purple line direct). Great compromise between cost and centrality.
areasToStay.description: Commercial core around pedestrian Rua de Santa Catarina, Mercado do Bolhão, and major metro intersections. Central, flat-ish terrain and great for budget-conscious groups.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Bolhão metro (direct airport line); São Bento station 10 min walk; major bus corridors along Av. dos Aliados and Rua de Santa Catarina.
areasToStay.reviews: Commended for convenience and value; some traffic noise reported on lower floors of main avenues.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Busy and safe during the day; after shops close the area quiets down but remains secure due to CCTV and foot traffic near metro hubs.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Fewer chain hotels here; Booking.com lists many apartments and B&Bs under ‘Cedofeita’. Ideal for groups who self-cater or want kitchen access.
areasToStay.description: Hip neighborhood with galleries, craft beer bars, and alternative nightlife. Young crowd, street art, and cooler evenings. Good for the ‘nightlife’ theme without the Ribeira tourist crush.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Carolina Michaelis or Lapa metro stations (10 min walk); frequent buses down Rua de Cedofeita; 15 min downhill walk to Ribeira.
areasToStay.reviews: Popular with creative travellers; praised for local vibe, noted as slightly hilly.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Considered safe; weekend nightlife brings crowds but less aggressive than typical party zones. Park areas west of Rua de Cedofeita can be quiet after dark—stick to the main gallery strip.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Properties on Booking.com often offer larger family/group rooms here versus old-town stock. Look for deals with parking if renting a car, though metro is sufficient.
areasToStay.description: Modern avenue known for contemporary architecture, wider sidewalks, and lower-density lodging. Smart mid-range option with excellent road/metro links to Matosinhos beach.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Casa da Música metro (direct airport line); bus 500 to Matosinhos beach (15 min); yellow line to city centre (5–7 min).
areasToStay.reviews: Valued by repeat visitors for calm and room size; minor complaint about distance from old-town buzz (easily solved by metro).
areasToStay.safetyTips: One of Porto’s safest districts; well-lit and active until late due to restaurants and offices. Standard caution at Casa da Música metro plaza.

areasToStay.bookingTips: Booking.com inventory is thinner here than central Porto—book early. Choose near Praia dos Ingleses for true beach access. Splurge-worthy for the final nights if budget allows.
areasToStay.description: Upscale Atlantic-front neighborhood at the river mouth. Sandy beaches, promenade jogging paths, and chic seafood restaurants. Best for a relaxed, beach-nearby finale before departure.
areasToStay.nearbyTransport: Tram line 1 along the river to Ribeira (scenic but slow); bus 500 along the coast; Uber/rideshare 10–15 min to Boavista/Casa da Música.
areasToStay.reviews: Lauded for tranquility and sea breeze; reviewers note you will ride transit or pay for rideshares to reach core Porto nightlife.
areasToStay.safetyTips: Affluent and very safe; evening promenade is busy with runners and diners. Remote from late-night club districts, which reduces nightlife risk but requires taxi back from downtown parties.

accommodationResearch.liveDataCaveats: Rates are estimated seasonal ranges for September 2026 derived from search context and typical Booking.com pricing patterns; actual live availability and nightly rates may vary. Always confirm on Booking.com before committing.
accommodationResearch.notes: Group of 3 friends with food/beaches/nightlife themes and moderate pace. Lisbon areas prioritized for transit to Alfama food activities and Bairro Alto nightlife. Porto areas prioritized for Matosinhos beach access and safe late-night returns.

flightResearch.caveats: All fares are estimates derived from web-search context for a multi-city/open-jaw London→Lisbon / Porto→London trip in September 2026. Live prices, schedules, and seat availability will vary. Low-cost carriers typically charge separately for checked baggage (approx EUR 25–45 per bag) and seat selection. National carriers usually include a 23 kg checked bag. Book directly via the listed providers and reconfirm times within 24 hours of purchase.
flightResearch.notes: Route matches the approved itinerary: arrive Lisbon 2026-09-10; depart Porto 2026-09-17. All options assume one-way/multi-city bookings rather than a round-trip to a single city.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Personal item included; cabin bag + checked bag cost extra (est. EUR 25–45 each per leg).
sample_options.label: Cheapest Low-Cost Combo
sample_options.layovers: Direct each leg (London area to Lisbon; Porto to London area)
sample_options.recommendation: Best for tight budgets. Pack light. Watch out for added fees and strict carry-on rules. Consider travel insurance due to limited schedule protection.
sample_options.route: London STN/LTN/LGW → Lisbon (LIS) / Porto (OPO) → London STN/LTN/LGW
sample_options.tradeoff: Lowest absolute cost, but sparse customer service, extra fees for bags/seats, and potential for airport transfers to secondary London airports.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Generally includes 1× 23 kg checked bag and carry-on; seat selection may cost extra on lowest fare tier.
sample_options.label: Fastest Direct Value
sample_options.recommendation: Strong default choice. Direct flights save time, Star Alliance reliability, and bags included. Competitive price for full-service.
sample_options.route: London LHR/LGW → Lisbon (LIS) / Porto (OPO) → London LHR/LGW
sample_options.tradeoff: Mid-range fare; slightly higher than low-cost but removes bag/seat headaches and gets you there faster without connections.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Personal item included; 1× cabin bag usually extra; checked bag extra. Mixed-carrier booking.
sample_options.label: Best Timing
sample_options.recommendation: Ideal if you want maximum usable time on the ground. Early arrival into Lisbon gives Day 1 back; late return from Porto avoids rushing on departure day.
sample_options.route: London LGW → Lisbon (LIS) early morning / Porto (OPO) → London STN late evening
sample_options.tradeoff: Two separate bookings to manage; baggage limits differ between carriers. Penalty for schedule changes falls on you, not a single airline.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Includes 1× 23 kg checked bag, carry-on, and complimentary seat selection on many economy fares. Higher tiers add flexibility.
sample_options.label: Best Comfort / Directness
sample_options.recommendation: Best for groups that want schedule security, generous baggage allowance, and easy rebooking if plans change. Worth the splurge if the budget allows.
sample_options.route: London LHR → Lisbon (LIS) / Porto (OPO) → London LHR
sample_options.tradeoff: Highest fare of the standard options, but delivers consistency and Heathrow convenience. Fees apply on hand-bag-only basic fares—double-check the fare class.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Personal item included; everything else is à la carte. Expect approx EUR 30–50 for a 20 kg checked bag per leg.
sample_options.label: Nearby-Airport Budget Alternative
sample_options.recommendation: Worth comparing if your group lives north of London or can accept the Luton journey. Savings are meaningful for 3 people.
sample_options.route: London LTN → Lisbon (LIS) / Porto (OPO) → London LTN
sample_options.tradeoff: Cheapest headline price, but London Luton requires longer/costlier ground transit than Heathrow or Gatwick; limited onboard comfort; strict bag policing.

sample_options.baggage_caveats: Depends on carrier chosen for shifted date. If TAP: bag usually included. If easyJet: bag extra.
sample_options.label: Flexible-Date / Adjacent-Day Alternative
sample_options.recommendation: Check departing Sep 9 evening and/or returning Sep 18 morning. Often lowers the multi-city total without eating much trip time. Useful if your group can wiggle dates.
sample_options.route: London area → Lisbon / Porto → London area
sample_options.tradeoff: Requires an extra night of accommodation (Sep 9 or Sep 17) but can unlock better slots or marginal fare savings on popular September weekends.
